    Added reflection to aim 8affbc01-11e0-4fa6-82aa-1723b7cd6168

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• I turned Open Source Release Prep into a real work queue and added 13 concrete sub-aims across startup, docs, repo hygiene,
  runtime simplification, and local .bowman project UX. The most immediate ones now exist as discrete aims instead of living only
  in the umbrella branch.

  I also completed two of those new aims.

  First, I fixed the frontend endpoint wiring so build-mode no longer assumes browser-side localhost for backend, broker, or
  spawned watchdog sessions. The new helper is in packages/frontend/src/utils/runtime-config.ts, and it is used from packages/
  frontend/src/trpc.ts, packages/frontend/src/trpc-watchdog.ts, and packages/frontend/src/stores/watchdog.ts. I verified that with
  npm run type-check -w frontend and npm run build-only -w frontend.

  Second, I added missing package-level docs for the core runtime pieces:
  packages/backend/README.md
  packages/shared/README.md
  packages/wrapped-agents/broker/README.md

  The next high-value aims are now clear:

  - Verify clean-clone dev/start flows end-to-end
  - Add preferred-port fallback and resolved-port reporting
  - Audit repository metadata and public-facing package manifests
  - Document .bowman data layout and git interaction clearly

  If you want, I’ll take the port-fallback/runtime-reporting aim next.
